name: Microwave Black Bean Burritos
recipeIngredient: 1 small Jalapeno pepper, seeded and finely diced
16 ounces Black beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
Info: posted by Perry Lowell, April 1993 from Good Food magazine, October 1988
This recipe gets 30% or less of its calories from fat. This is not a low sodium recipe, however eliminating added salt can reduce sodium content. Makes 2 servings.
Garnishes: shredded romaine lettuce, sliced red onion, grated Monterey Jack cheese, and sour cream.
1. Stir oil, onion, jalapeno, and garlic together in a 9-inch glass pie plate. Cover with plastic wrap, leaving one corner open for venting.
2. Microwave on high power for 1 minute. Stir in cumin, oregano, chili powder, and ground coriander; microwave, covered and vented, for 1 minute.
3. Stir in beans and water; microwave, covered and vented, for 2 minutes. Transfer half of the bean mixture to a blender or food processor and puree. Stir back into the remaining beans. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
4. Combine avocado, tomato, scallion, coriander, lime juice, and zest in a small bowl. Season salsa to taste with salt and pepper.
5. When ready to serve, microwave the beans, covered and vented, until very hot, 1-3 minutes. Serve beans with tortillas, salsa, and garnishes.
